what_it_does: Defines a standalone HTML preview card for the Vi AI framework mark, showing a rotated diamond logo with gold line-fill, explanatory copy, and compact metadata.
observations: Includes a dsCard metadata comment with Brand group, card name, subtitle, and 700x240 viewport.
Links shared card styling from _card.css and adds file-local CSS for the mark and text layout.
Uses a two-column grid card with the diamond mark on the left and description/meta content on the right.
The mark is a 120px square rotated 45 degrees, bordered in accent gold, with a subtle repeating gold line pattern.
The inner Vi text is rotated back -45 degrees and uses display typography with a gold styled i.
Highlights User Portal, Property Wizard, and Virtual Hubs names in gold within the descriptive paragraph.
Includes a metadata row for shape, pattern, and wordmark.
standouts: Diamond mark construction is done with CSS rotation rather than an image asset.
The gold line-fill pattern is encoded directly with repeating-linear-gradient.
The file combines visual specimen, usage description, and token-like metadata in one preview card.
conventions: Uses CSS custom properties for color, typography, shadows, and surface styling.
Keeps preview-specific CSS inline in the HTML file.
Follows a card-preview pattern with a dsCard annotation, shared stylesheet link, and compact visual sample.
Uses semantic text elements for heading, paragraph, and metadata, with visual styling handled in CSS and inline styles.
concerns: The copy describes the i as subscripted, while the .vi-text .i rule sets vertical-align: super.
The i styling is split between a CSS class and an inline style, with the inline font-size differing from the class rule.
The left preview wrapper uses inline layout styles rather than the local stylesheet.
notes: A compact brand-specimen card for the Vi diamond wordmark, pairing the CSS-built logo with descriptive usage notes.
questions: Should the gold i be visually subscripted or superscripted?
Is the inline i font-size intended to override the .vi-text .i rule?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it presents the Vi AI framework mark as a diamond with line-fill and a gold i, though the subscript wording conflicts with the vertical-align styling.
intention_signals: It seems intended to document and preview the Vi framework brand mark in a reusable design-system card format.
novelty: The logo is represented as a CSS-built rotated diamond with an internal counter-rotated wordmark and patterned fill instead of relying on an external image.
contribution: Contributes a clear Vi brand-mark specimen: diamond geometry, gold line-fill treatment, wordmark styling, contextual product associations, and concise metadata that a fused system would preserve as brand-design substrate.
summary_for_embedding: Standalone HTML design-system preview card for the Vi AI framework mark, showing a CSS diamond logo with gold line-fill, wordmark, explanatory copy, and metadata.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for documenting and visually presenting the Vi brand mark within a design-system preview surface.
